title: satureja ericifolia subsp. hierrensis
intro: Satureja ericifolia subsp. hierrensis, also known as Hierren's savory, is a subspecies of the Satureja ericifolia plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family, which is native to the Canary Islands. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Hierren's savory include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce respiratory problems, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health concerns. Hierren's savory can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in culinary applications, adding flavor to various dishes while providing potential health benefits.
